name: Milo Minderbinder
creator: Joseph Heller
practice: War profiteering, supply chain manipulation, the commodification of everything
approach: Everything is a commodity, including the war itself. His syndicate 'M&M Enterprises' — in which 'everyone has a share' — buys and sells everything, including bombing his own squadron for profit. The logic of the market applied without constraint becomes indistinguishable from insanity.
body_of_work: The mess officer who becomes a war profiteer. Buys Egyptian cotton and contracts to bomb his own base. Everyone has a share in the syndicate, but the shares are worthless. He's the purest expression of fiscal logic divorced from human consequence.
why_instructive: He's the anti-pattern for the fiscal layer. When money flows without human accountability, you get Milo. The dome's fiscal layer must always connect spending to human outcomes — the anti-Milo.

dome_relevance.3: Fiscal — money divorced from purpose, the anti-pattern

sphere_relevance.4: Economic — market logic without ethical constraint
